## Get a list of patrons with non-blocking notes. Pulls the patron ID, barcode, name, note text, and last updated date
**Source:** CyberpunkLibrarian/Patrons/Patrons With Non Blocking Notes.sql  
**Complexity:** MSSQL (Admin/SSMS)  
**Tables:** PatronNotes, Patrons, PatronRegistration  

```sql
/* Get a list of patrons with non-blocking notes. Pulls the patron ID, barcode, name, note text, and last updated date */

SELECT
    pn.PatronID AS "Patron ID",
    p.Barcode AS "Barcode",
    pr.PatronFullName AS "Name",
    pn.NonBlockingStatusNotes as "Notes",
    pn.NonBlockingStatusNoteDate AS "Last Noted"

FROM
    Polaris.Polaris.PatronNotes pn WITH (NOLOCK)

INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.Patrons p WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.PatronID = pn.PatronID
INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.PatronRegistration pr WITH (NOLOCK) ON pr.PatronID = pn.PatronID

WHERE
    DATALENGTH(pn.NonBlockingStatusNotes) > 2
    
ORDER BY
    pn.NonBlockingStatusNoteDate DESC
```

## Pull a list of patrons with blocking notes. Gives you the patron ID, barcode, name, note text, and last updated date
**Source:** CyberpunkLibrarian/Patrons/Patrons with Blocking Notes.sql  
**Complexity:** MSSQL (Admin/SSMS)  
**Tables:** PatronNotes, Patrons, PatronRegistration  

```sql
/* Pull a list of patrons with blocking notes. Gives you the patron ID, barcode, name, note text, and last updated date */

SELECT
    pn.PatronID AS "Patron ID",
    p.Barcode AS "Barcode",
    pr.PatronFullName AS "Name",
    pn.BlockingStatusNotes as "Notes",
    pn.BlockingStatusNoteDate AS "Last Noted"

FROM
    Polaris.Polaris.PatronNotes pn WITH (NOLOCK)

INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.Patrons p WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.PatronID = pn.PatronID
INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.PatronRegistration pr WITH (NOLOCK) ON pr.PatronID = pn.PatronID

WHERE
    DATALENGTH(pn.BlockingStatusNotes) > 2
ORDER BY
    pn.NonBlockingStatusNoteDate DESC
```

## This query is a sort-of reverse process from the Deleted Items Billed and Overdue query. Instead of looking for deleted items and the patrons they're attached to, this query looks for *patrons* and the deleted items attached to *them.*
**Source:** CyberpunkLibrarian/Patrons/Patrons with Deleted Items Billed and Overdue.sql  
**Complexity:** MSSQL (Admin/SSMS)  
**Tables:** the, PatronAccount, Patrons, PatronRegistration, FeeReasonCodes, PatronAcctDeletedItemRecords  

```sql
/* This query is a sort-of reverse process from the Deleted Items Billed and Overdue
query. Instead of looking for deleted items and the patrons they're attached to,
this query looks for *patrons* and the deleted items attached to *them.* */

SELECT
	pr.PatronFullName AS [Patron Name],
	p.Barcode AS [Patron Barcode],
	padir.BrowseTitle AS [Title],
	padir.Barcode AS [Item Barcode],
	frc.FeeDescription AS [Fee Description]
FROM
	Polaris.Polaris.PatronAccount pa WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN
	Polaris.Polaris.Patrons p WITH (NOLOCK)
	ON (p.PatronID = pa.PatronID)
INNER JOIN
	Polaris.Polaris.PatronRegistration pr WITH (NOLOCK)
	ON (pr.PatronID = pa.PatronID)
INNER JOIN
	Polaris.Polaris.FeeReasonCodes frc WITH (NOLOCK)
	ON (frc.FeeReasonCodeID = pa.FeeReasonCodeID)
INNER JOIN
	Polaris.Polaris.PatronAcctDeletedItemRecords padir WITH (NOLOCK)
	ON (padir.ItemRecordID = pa.ItemRecordID)
WHERE -- Look for patrons with outstanding balances
	pa.TxnID IN (
		SELECT DISTINCT TxnID 
		FROM Polaris.Polaris.PatronAccount WITH (NOLOCK)
		WHERE TxnCodeID IN (1) 
		AND OutstandingAmount > 0)
AND -- 0 = Overdue and -1 = Replacement Cost
	pa.FeeReasonCodeID IN (-1,0)
ORDER BY
	pr.PatronFullName,
	padir.BrowseTitle
```

---
## These queries work together to get email addresses for patrons who've checked out certain numbers of items over a given period of time. In my case we used this for an email marketing campaign where patrons who'd frequently used their cards got a different message than patrons who only used their car
**Source:** CyberpunkLibrarian/Patrons/Pull Email List for Patrons Based on Circ Activity.sql  
**Complexity:** MSSQL (Admin/SSMS)  
**Tables:** TransactionHeaders, the, PatronRegistration, Patrons, Organizations, TempPatronCircCounts  

```sql
/* These queries work together to get email addresses for patrons who've checked out certain numbers of
items over a given period of time. In my case we used this for an email marketing campaign where patrons
who'd frequently used their cards got a different message than patrons who only used their cards
occasionally.

There are two queries here, one which complies patron circulation data to a table in your Polaris database
and another than queries that table. You can eventually drop the patron circulation table later on if you 
like. It's just a lot faster to have it sitting in your database rather than using the same data in a temp
table over and over again. */


-- FIRST QUERY - RUN ONCE, USE AS NEEDED, DROP AFTERWARDS --

/* This is the first query that collects and writes your patron circulation data to a table in your
Polaris database. This query can take some time to run depending on the amount of circulation data you
need. However, once that data is in the table, it's very fast to query and use. You can drop this
table later on whenever you're done using it. */

SELECT
    patron.numValue AS PatronID,
    COUNT(DISTINCT th.TransactionID) AS CircCount
INTO -- CHANGE THIS TABLE NAME TO SUIT YOUR NEEDS
    Polaris.Lss.TempPatronCircCounts
FROM
    PolarisTransactions.Polaris.TransactionHeaders th WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN -- Pulls the PatronID
    PolarisTransactions.Polaris.TransactionDetails patron WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON (th.TransactionID = patron.TransactionID AND patron.TransactionSubTypeID = 6)
WHERE -- Checkouts
    TransactionTypeID = 6001
AND -- Adjust dates to suit your needs -- see line 76 below.
    TranClientDate BETWEEN '2023-01-01 00:00:00.000' AND '2024-02-29 23:59:59.999'
GROUP BY
    patron.numValue

-- SECOND QUERY - DATA DELIVERY --

/* This is the query that pulls data from the table you created above and will deliver email addresses
for patrons whose circulation usage meets your criteria */


-- Create a tempoary table to limit our range and optimize the query
CREATE TABLE #TempPatronsAndEmails (
    PatronID INT,
    Abbreviation NVARCHAR(15),
    EmailAddress NVARCHAR(64),
    NameLast NVARCHAR(100),
    PatronFullName NVARCHAR(100)
);

-- Populate that table
INSERT INTO #TempPatronsAndEmails

SELECT
    pr.PatronID,
    o.Abbreviation,
    pr.EmailAddress,
    pr.NameLast,
    pr.PatronFullName
FROM
    Polaris.Polaris.PatronRegistration pr WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.Patrons p WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON (p.PatronID = pr.PatronID)
INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.Organizations o WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON (o.OrganizationID = p.OrganizationID)
WHERE -- We wanted cards that were of a certain age. Adjust your WHERE and AND clauses as needed
    RegistrationDate < DATEADD(MONTH,-3,GETDATE())
AND -- This query was directed at RCLS in Riverside, CA. However you can change this as desired.
    p.OrganizationID IN (
        SELECT OrganizationID
        FROM Polaris.Polaris.Organizations WITH (NOLOCK)
        WHERE Name LIKE 'RCLS%'
    )
AND -- No reason to deal with patrons who don't have an email address at all.
    pr.EmailAddress IS NOT NULL
AND -- Don't include patrons where LastActivityDate doesn't fit in with the parameters. These dates should match the dates in the first query.
    p.LastActivityDate BETWEEN '2023-01-01 00:00:00.000' AND '2024-02-29 23:59:59.999'
ORDER BY
    RegistrationDate DESC;

-- Now that we have a working subset of patrons, use the temp table to pull your emails.
SELECT
    tpe.PatronID,
    tpe.Abbreviation,
    tpe.EmailAddress,
    tpe.NameLast,
    tpe.PatronFullName,
    tpcc.CircCount
FROM
    #TempPatronsAndEmails tpe
LEFT JOIN
    Polaris.Lss.TempPatronCircCounts tpcc WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON (tpcc.PatronID = tpe.PatronID)
WHERE -- Set this as needed for your circulation parameters
    tpcc.CircCount > 12
ORDER BY
    tpe.Abbreviation,
    tpe.PatronFullName

-- Tidy up
DROP TABLE #TempPatronsAndEmails
```

## This is a statistical query that pulls a list of patrons along with their last checkout date. This allows for checking on circ based patron activity rather than relying on the Last Activity Date. Because the Last Activity Date can get updated for any number of reasons that have nothing to do with a 
**Source:** CyberpunkLibrarian/Patrons/Pull Patrons Last Checkout Date.sql  
**Complexity:** MSSQL (Admin/SSMS)  
**Tables:** TransactionHeaders, TransactionDetails, Patrons, Organizations  

```sql
/*
This is a statistical query that pulls a list of patrons along with their last checkout date. This allows
for checking on circ based patron activity rather than relying on the Last Activity Date. Because
the Last Activity Date can get updated for any number of reasons that have nothing to do with a circulation
based transaction. You can limit the date to a given timeframe if needed or you can change the
TransactionTypeID to something else if you wish to track that.
*/

SELECT
    DISTINCT(p.PatronID),
    o.[Name],
    MAX(th.TranClientDate) AS LastCheckOutDate

FROM
    PolarisTransactions.Polaris.TransactionHeaders th WITH (NOLOCK)

INNER JOIN
    PolarisTransactions.Polaris.TransactionDetails td WITH (NOLOCK) ON td.TransactionID = th.TransactionID
INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.Patrons p WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.PatronID = td.numValue
INNER JOIN
    Polaris.Polaris.Organizations o WITH (NOLOCK) ON o.OrganizationID = p.OrganizationID

WHERE
    th.TransactionTypeID = 6001 -- Check out transaction
AND
    td.TransactionSubTypeID = 6 -- PatronID in the TransactionDetails table
AND
    p.OrganizationID in (5) -- Limit by specific org codes if needed.

GROUP BY
    p.PatronID,
    o.[Name]
HAVING
    MAX(th.TranClientDate) < '2018-04-01 00:00:00.000' -- Set date parameters as desired

ORDER BY
    LastCheckOutDate DESC
```

## Compare PatronCode Circulation Policies — Which Codes Have Identical Loan Limits?
**Source:** RHPL Staff Research (2026-04-03)
**Complexity:** MSSQL (Admin/SSMS)
**Tables:** MaterialLoanLimits, MaterialTypes, PatronCodes
**Key insight:** Use `MaterialLoanLimits` (the actual policy configuration table) — NOT `PatronRegistration`. `PatronRegistration` fields like `ExtendedLoanPeriods` and `IncreasedCheckOutLimits` are per-patron individual overrides, not the patron-code-level policy. `MaterialLoanLimits` stores MaxItems and MaxRequestItems per PatronCode + MaterialType combination, scoped by OrganizationID.

```sql
-- Step 1: Confirm your OrganizationID
-- SELECT OrganizationID, Name FROM Polaris.Polaris.Organizations ORDER BY OrganizationID;

-- Step 2: Compare PatronCode loan limit profiles — each row = one group of matching codes
WITH MaterialProfile AS (
    SELECT
        mll.PatronCodeID,
        STRING_AGG(
            CAST(mll.MaterialTypeID AS varchar(10)) + ':' +
            CAST(mll.MaxItems AS varchar(10)) + '/' +
            CAST(mll.MaxRequestItems AS varchar(10)),
            '|'
        ) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY mll.MaterialTypeID) AS LimitProfile
    FROM Polaris.Polaris.MaterialLoanLimits mll
    WHERE mll.PatronCodeID IN (1,2,3,4,6,7,8,9,14,15,17,19,20,22,24,25,26,27,29)
      AND mll.OrganizationID = 3  -- RHPL OrganizationID
    GROUP BY mll.PatronCodeID
)
SELECT
    STRING_AGG(CAST(PatronCodeID AS varchar(10)), ', ')
        WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY PatronCodeID) AS MatchingPatronCodes,
    COUNT(*)                                  AS GroupSize,
    LimitProfile
FROM MaterialProfile
GROUP BY LimitProfile
ORDER BY GroupSize DESC, LimitProfile;
```

```sql
-- Step 3 (enhanced): Compare physical AND digital loan limits separately
-- Classifies material types by description keywords; adjust LIKE patterns for your system
-- First run: SELECT MaterialTypeID, Description FROM Polaris.Polaris.MaterialTypes ORDER BY MaterialTypeID
-- to verify which IDs are digital at RHPL.
WITH TypedLimits AS (
    SELECT
        mll.PatronCodeID,
        mll.MaterialTypeID,
        mll.MaxItems,
        mll.MaxRequestItems,
        CASE
            WHEN mt.Description LIKE '%eBook%'
              OR mt.Description LIKE '%eAudio%'
              OR mt.Description LIKE '%eVideo%'
              OR mt.Description LIKE '%eMagazine%'
              OR mt.Description LIKE '%Digital%'
              OR mt.Description LIKE '%Online%'
              OR mt.Description LIKE '%Streaming%'
              OR mt.Description LIKE '%Download%'
            THEN 'Digital'
            ELSE 'Physical'
        END AS MediaCategory
    FROM Polaris.Polaris.MaterialLoanLimits mll
    JOIN Polaris.Polaris.MaterialTypes mt ON mll.MaterialTypeID = mt.MaterialTypeID
    WHERE mll.PatronCodeID IN (1,2,3,4,6,7,8,9,14,15,17,19,20,22,24,25,26,27,29)
      AND mll.OrganizationID = 3  -- RHPL OrganizationID
),
PhysicalProfile AS (
    SELECT PatronCodeID,
        STRING_AGG(
            CAST(MaterialTypeID AS varchar(10)) + ':' +
            CAST(MaxItems AS varchar(10)) + '/' +
            CAST(MaxRequestItems AS varchar(10)),
            '|'
        ) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY MaterialTypeID) AS PhysProfile
    FROM TypedLimits WHERE MediaCategory = 'Physical'
    GROUP BY PatronCodeID
),
DigitalProfile AS (
    SELECT PatronCodeID,
        STRING_AGG(
            CAST(MaterialTypeID AS varchar(10)) + ':' +
            CAST(MaxItems AS varchar(10)) + '/' +
            CAST(MaxRequestItems AS varchar(10)),
            '|'
        ) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY MaterialTypeID) AS DigProfile
    FROM TypedLimits WHERE MediaCategory = 'Digital'
    GROUP BY PatronCodeID
),
Combined AS (
    SELECT
        p.PatronCodeID,
        p.PhysProfile,
        ISNULL(d.DigProfile, '(none)') AS DigProfile
    FROM PhysicalProfile p
    LEFT JOIN DigitalProfile d ON p.PatronCodeID = d.PatronCodeID
)
SELECT
    STRING_AGG(CAST(PatronCodeID AS varchar(10)), ', ')
        WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY PatronCodeID) AS MatchingPatronCodes,
    COUNT(*) AS GroupSize,
    PhysProfile AS PhysicalLimitProfile,
    DigProfile  AS DigitalLimitProfile
FROM Combined
GROUP BY PhysProfile, DigProfile
ORDER BY GroupSize DESC, PhysProfile;
```

**Prompt template for asking the AI to write this type of query:**
> I want to compare PatronCode-level circulation **policy configuration** — not individual patron data. Query `MaterialLoanLimits` (columns: PatronCodeID, MaterialTypeID, MaxItems, MaxRequestItems, OrganizationID) — do NOT use PatronRegistration or aggregate patron records. Group PatronCodeIDs that have **identical full profiles** onto the **same output row** using STRING_AGG. Include OrganizationID = [your org] filter. PatronCodeIDs: 1,2,3,4,6,7,8,9,14,15,17,19,20,22,24,25,26,27,29.
